Home/Posting

Where To Post

Separate official notices from community visibility.

Small communities have physical boards, public offices, digital public-notice pages, calendars, newsletters, and informal partner channels. Use this page to verify the right channel without treating every board as an ad space.

Mobile quick route

Post an event

Use this when the thing has a date, place, deadline, class, show, fundraiser, or public meeting.

Event posting hubRaton eventsTrinidad eventsHuerfano eventsSubmit update

Posting map

Physical plus digital channels to verify by community.

Search the rows in this section.

CommunityPhysical pathwayDigital pathwayUse forSource
Trinidad / Las AnimasCity Hall and Clerk pathway at 135 N. Animas Street, plus public places used for notices under local rules.City public notices, agendas, minutes, Clerk pages, emergency alerts, chamber/tourism event pages, and Visit Trinidad resources.Official notices, event source check, civic updates, tourism calendar submissions, and public-sector timing checks.Open page
Walsenburg / HuerfanoCity Hall entrance at 525 S. Albert Avenue, County Clerk and Recorder, and courthouse bulletin-board/public-office pathways.Walsenburg agendas/minutes, Huerfano County public notices, county news, meeting pages, calendars, and newsletter/update pages.Public notices, meeting awareness, business-license context, county calendar checks, and civic program visibility.Open page
Raton / ColfaxCity Hall posting cabinet and Colfax County Clerk/County buildings including 333 Savage Avenue and other public-office pathways.City agendas/minutes/events calendar/public notices/bids/updates; Colfax County news, calendar, legal notices, and public hearings.Official notices, public hearing awareness, county business context, community calendar timing, and source check.Open page
General regional patternLibraries, chambers, venues, schools, visitor centers, coffee shops, galleries, and community centers may have boards.Official websites, tourism calendars, chamber pages, newspaper/radio pages, Facebook/community groups, and partner newsletters.Supplemental community visibility after the official or owner-controlled channel has been checked.Local check needed

Use "field-check needed" entries as details to confirm, not as a claim that posting is already allowed.

Posting method

A simple sequence for offline and online visibility.

1

Verify the owner

Identify who controls the board, calendar, newsletter, or directory before preparing materials.

2

Match the purpose

Official notice, public event, business listing, and paid advertisement are different requests.

3

Use clear assets

Keep a short blurb, accessible flyer, square image, date, location, and contact ready.

4

Track proof

Record where it was posted, who approved it, when it expires, and what response it produced.

Next action

Choose the next practical route.

  • Find event calendars, newsletters, visitor guides, and directory channels
  • Use copy-ready outreach templates
  • Submit a corrected posting route
  • Search public directories and local leads

Choose the event route

Do not post every event the same way.

The right first channel depends on whether the event is artistic, commercial, civic, nonprofit, educational, visitor-facing, or official.

Event typeUse firstInclude
Art opening or gallery receptionGallery, arts, tourism, venue, and media channelsArtist names, reception time, image, sales/viewing notes.
Live musicVenue lineup, tourism calendar, local media, performer pagesPerformer, genre, set time, ticket/free status, age limit.
FundraiserPartner orgs, community calendars, nonprofit networks, local mediaPublic benefit, beneficiary, donation/registration path.
Class or workshopLibraries, galleries, schools, community centers, calendarsAge range, cost, registration, materials, accessibility.
Public meeting or listening sessionOfficial municipal/civic channels and partner organizationsWhether it is official, educational, public comment, or informal.
Business launchChamber, city/economic-development, visitor routes, local mediaOpening date, public offer, hours, location, and contact route.

Immediate directory

Find the right route.

Search by need, county, town, audience, or task. Results include practical links and update reminders so users can act without treating old details as final.

Results update after submitting the form or after a short pause while typing. Press Escape to close this panel.

Colfax NM + Las Animas CO + Huerfano CO

This guide helps businesses, artists, nonprofits, programs, venues, and service providers find the right regional route faster.

See something outdated? Send a source link through Submit Update so the guide can improve.
